memoryArtifacts.headline: The original lists of persons of quality; emigrants; religious exiles; by Hotten, John Camden pg 48-49
Will of Simon Tuttle of Ringstead, Northamptonshire, England, made 1627 but not proved until after his death in 1630.
memoryArtifacts.description:
Names only 4 children, all sons, in this order of age:
RICHARD, JOHN, THOMAS (dec.) and WILLIAM. No daughters mentioned at all. Wife Isabel is not mentioned but she is proven by an emigration record with her sons.

zh.Ringstead, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾林斯亞德
zh.Tharston, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可塔爾斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Wells of Ringstead於1565年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾林斯亞德時，她的父親John Wells of Ringstead22歲，母親Hellene of Ringstead26歲。她於1592年6月25日與Simon Tuttle在英國諾福爾可塔爾斯頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1635年4月6日之後。

zh-hans.Ringstead, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾林斯亞德
zh-hans.Tharston, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可塔爾斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Wells of Ringstead于1565年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾林斯亞德时，她的父亲John Wells of Ringstead22岁，母亲Hellene of Ringstead26岁。她于1592年6月25日与Simon Tuttle在英国諾福爾可塔爾斯頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1635年4月6日之后。

nameMeanings.name: Wells
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Wells next the Sea (Norfolk) or Wells (Somerset), both named with the plural of Old English wella ‘spring, stream’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a group of springs or streams.
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dupuis   ‘from the well’.
History: One of numerous early immigrants from England bearing this name was Thomas Welles, governor of colonial CT, who was in Hartford, CT, by 1636.

variants.name: Dupuis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Pierre, Armand, Cecile, Normand, Emile, Lucien, Marcel, Rosaire, Alphonse, Henri.
topographic name for someone ‘from the well’, from Old French puis ‘well’, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’. Compare Dupee , Dupuie , Dupuy 2, and Wells .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Wells of Ringstead was born in 1565, in Ringstead, Northamptonshire, England, her father, John Wells of Ringstead, was 22 and her mother, Hellene of Ringstead, was 26. She married Simon Tuttle on 25 June 1592, in Tharston, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died after 6 April 1635.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Wells of Ringstead est née en 1565, à Ringstead, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, John Wells of Ringstead, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Hellene of Ringstead, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Simon Tuttle le 25 juin 1592, à Tharston, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée après 6 avril 1635.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Wells of Ringstead 1565 in Ringstead, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Wells of Ringstead 22 und ihre Mutter Hellene of Ringstead 26. Sie hat am 25. Juni 1592 in Tharston, Norfolk, England Simon Tuttle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb nach 6. April 1635.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Wells of Ringstead è nata il nel 1565 nel Ringstead, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, John Wells of Ringstead, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Hellene of Ringstead, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Simon Tuttle il 25 giugno 1592, nel Tharston, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì dopo il 6 aprile 1635.

ja.Ringstead, Northamptonshire, England: イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアリングステアド
ja.Tharston, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクサルストン
ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Wells of Ringsteadが1565年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアリングステアドで生まれた時，父John Wells of Ringsteadは22歳，母Hellene of Ringsteadは26歳であった。1592年6月25日にイギリスノーフォルクサルストンでSimon Tuttleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1635年4月6日以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Wells of Ringstead(이)가 1565년 영국 노르섬프토실르 링스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Wells of Ringstead(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hellene of Ringstead(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1592년 6월 25일 영국 노르폴크 스아르스턴에서 Simon Tuttle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1635년 4월 6일 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Wells of Ringstead nasceu em 1565, em Ringstead, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Wells of Ringstead, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Hellene of Ringstead, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Simon Tuttle em 25 de junho de 1592, em Tharston, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu após 6 de abril de 1635.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Wells of Ringstead родилась в 1565 г. в местности рингстид, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Wells of Ringstead было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Hellene of Ringstead, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Simon Tuttle 25 июня 1592 г. в местности тарстон, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла после 6 апрель 1635 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Wells of Ringstead nació en 1565, en Ringstead, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Wells of Ringstead, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Hellene of Ringstead, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Simon Tuttle el 25 de junio de 1592, en Tharston, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió después de 6 de abril de 1635.
